hypothesis: If catalytic surface shell compositions are encoded with a chemically informed autoencoder and sampled by a conditional latent diffusion model conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed elements after pretraining on OC20 and fine-tuning on HEA (including via LoRA), then CompGen will produce shell composition distributions that more closely match ground-truth (lower Fréchet Distance), better satisfy element constraints (lower Leakage), and achieve higher pointwise fidelity (higher Cosine similarity) than chemically-agnostic baselines while retaining performance with reduced fine-tuning data.
